role: Argentine federal prosecutor; lead investigator of the AMIA bombing case (2004–2015)
bio: Alberto Nisman was an Argentine federal prosecutor who spent more than a decade as the lead investigator into the [AMIA bombing](/events/amia-bombing-1994) of July 18, 1994 — the deadliest terrorist attack in Argentine history. Appointed in 2004 as the special prosecutor for the AMIA case, Nisman built a comprehensive evidentiary record identifying Iran's Supreme National Security Council as having authorised the attack and naming several Iranian officials and operatives including [Mohsen Rabbani](/people/mohsen-rabbani), [Hadi Soleimanpour](/people/hadi-soleimanpour), [Ahmad Reza Asghari](/people/ahmad-reza-asghari), and [Ali Fallahian](/people/ali-fallahian). In January 2015, Nisman filed a landmark charging document accusing Argentine President [Cristina Fernández de Kirchner](/people/cristina-fernandez-de-kirchner) and Foreign Minister [Héctor Timerman](/people/hector-timerman) of treason, alleging they had secretly agreed to shield Iranian suspects from prosecution in exchange for a trade deal with Iran — the [Iran–Argentina MOU](/events/iran-argentina-mou-2013) of 2013. On January 18, 2015 — the night before he was scheduled to testify before the Argentine Congress about those charges — Nisman was found dead in his apartment with a gunshot wound to the head. Argentine authorities initially ruled his death a suicide. Subsequent judicial investigations and forensic re-examinations raised serious doubts about that finding, and in 2019 a federal court determined he had been murdered. The exact perpetrators and chain of responsibility remain contested.

sources.title: Argentine prosecutor Alberto Nisman found dead

editorial_notes: Found dead January 18 2015. 2019 federal court finding: murder, not suicide. Filed charges against Kirchner and FM Timerman Jan 14 2015. Was due to testify before Congress Jan 19 2015.
